title: GitHub→AWS OIDC Deployment Identity — positive deployment role with exact repo/env/workflow claims
description: A platform engineer wires a GitHub Actions deployment workflow to assume an AWS deployment role. Hulumi's Edge Platform (v1.3.2) provides GitHubAwsOidcDeploymentRole, a positive cross-provider abstraction that generates an AWS IAM role whose trust policy binds the GitHub OIDC subject to exact repository, environment, and reusable-workflow (job_workflow_ref) claims — no wildcard sub — with least-privilege policy attachments. G_OIDC_1 is the regression guardrail that rejects wildcard/StringLike sub trust at preview time.
actors: Platform Engineer (trusted; provisions the deployment role)
Deployment Workflow (trusted within exact OIDC claims)
Compromised Workflow / Action (untrusted; attempts to assume the production role)
Contributor (semi-trusted; may omit environment gates or use long-lived keys)
assets: AWS IAM deployment role and its trust policy
GitHub OIDC subject claims (repository, environment, job_workflow_ref)
Least-privilege policy attachments on the deployment role
Production AWS resources reachable by the deployment role

stride.name: Wildcard sub trust assumed by a compromised workflow
stride.description: A compromised workflow or Action uses a wildcard / StringLike sub trust to assume the production AWS deployment role without the intended repository, environment, or reusable-workflow constraints. Mitigation: GitHubAwsOidcDeploymentRole binds exact repository + environment + job_workflow_ref claims; G_OIDC_1 rejects wildcard/StringLike sub conditions at preview time.

stride.name: Missing protected-environment gate lets any branch deploy
stride.description: A contributor omits the protected-environment gate for production, so any matching branch can assume the deployment role and deploy. Mitigation: the environment input is required for the startup-hardened tier so the trust is bound to a protected environment with reviewers/branch policy.

stride.name: Long-lived AWS keys used instead of OIDC
stride.description: Long-lived AWS access keys are added as GitHub secrets instead of using the short-lived OIDC role, bypassing the short-lived identity model. Mitigation: docs and a policy fixture reject a deployment-capable repository without the OIDC role path; the positive component makes the safe path the easy path.

stride.name: Over-scoped deployment role
stride.description: The deployment role is granted broader permissions than the deployment needs, so a compromised workflow within the exact claims still has excess blast radius. Mitigation: GitHubAwsOidcDeploymentRole uses least-privilege policy attachments scoped to the deployment targets, not account-wide writes.

stride.name: Deployment not attributable across providers
stride.description: A deployment cannot be attributed to a specific repository/workflow/actor across the GitHub→AWS boundary. Mitigation: the AWS IAM role and GitHub environment audit trails together attribute the assume to exact OIDC claims; the role is tagged so CloudTrail distinguishes IaC-originated assumes.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/platform-patterns.GitHubAwsOidcDeploymentRole
recommendedComponents.rationale: Positive AWS IAM role abstraction binding GitHub OIDC trust to exact repository, environment, and job_workflow_ref claims with least-privilege policy attachments and no wildcard sub.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/policies.github.G_OIDC_1
recommendedComponents.availability: Shipped in @hulumi/policies@1.3.2 (rule since v1.1.0 M3; covers the cross-provider deployment role)
recommendedComponents.rationale: Declarative CrossGuard rule rejecting StringLike / wildcard sub conditions on GitHub OIDC trust to AWS / Azure / GCP at preview time; the regression guardrail for the deployment role.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/baseline.github.OrgFoundation
recommendedComponents.rationale: Ships the org-side OIDC subject-claim customization template with the safe three-axis default so the GitHub side matches the exact-claim AWS trust.
